_meta.description: D6 fixtures for hermes / beautiful-chat (the flagship combined cell). Covers the five D5 probe pills: bar chart + pie chart (useComponent controlled gen-UI), schedule meeting (useHumanInTheLoop MeetingTimePicker), toggle theme (useFrontendTool), and search flights (A2UI fixed-schema FlightCard). Two divergences from langgraph-python's beautiful-chat.json, both mandated by the hermes AG-UI transport: (1) search_flights is a langgraph BACKEND tool that calls a2ui.render() and emits an a2ui_operations container; hermes has no A2UI backend and a generic aimock agent cannot emit an agent-side tool RESULT the middleware would detect, so the agent EMITS the middleware-injected render_a2ui directly with a flat FlightCard Row (identical to langgraph's _build_flight_components output) into the copilotkit://app-dashboard-catalog the page registers. (2) The chart legs match langgraph's D5 fixture 1:1 (barChart/pieChart useComponent, no query_data leg). Every entry carries context:hermes so it never collides with the langgraph-python beautiful-chat fixtures.

fixtures._comment: Bar Chart pill — 1st leg: emit the barChart useComponent tool call. Matches langgraph's D5 barChart fixture (data + title/description). Gated on hasToolResult:false so it fires on the tool-call turn.

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: bar chart of expenses by category

fixtures._comment: Bar Chart pill — 2nd leg: one-sentence narration after the barChart component renders (gated on the barChart tool result being present).

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: bar chart of expenses by category

response.content: Bar chart rendered above — Salaries dominate at $80k, with Rent at $15k as the next-largest category.

fixtures._comment: Pie Chart pill — 1st leg: emit the pieChart useComponent tool call. Fixture supplies 4 slices so the DonutChart renders 1 background + 4 slice circles (probe asserts >= 3 svg circles).

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: pie chart of revenue distribution by category

toolCalls.name: pieChart
toolCalls.arguments: {"title":"Revenue by Category","description":"Breakdown of revenue across product categories","data":[{"label":"Electronics","value":42000},{"label":"Clothing","value":28000},{"label":"Food","value":18000},{"label":"Books","value":12000}]}

fixtures._comment: Pie Chart pill — 2nd leg: narration after the pieChart renders.

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: pie chart of revenue distribution by category

response.content: Pie chart rendered above — Electronics leads at $42k, followed by Clothing, Food, and Books.

fixtures._comment: Schedule Meeting pill — 1st leg: emit the scheduleTime useHumanInTheLoop tool call. The frontend renders MeetingTimePicker and PAUSES the agent until the user clicks a slot; the probe clicks the default 'Tomorrow' slot which resolves the HITL via respond().

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: schedule a 30-minute meeting to learn about CopilotKit

fixtures._comment: Schedule Meeting pill — 2nd leg: after the user picks a slot the HITL resolves and feeds the choice back; emit a closing confirmation (gated on the scheduleTime result being present).

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: schedule a 30-minute meeting to learn about CopilotKit

response.content: Meeting scheduled — calendar invite is on its way.

fixtures._comment: Toggle Theme pill — 1st leg: emit the toggleTheme useFrontendTool call (empty args). The client handler flips document.documentElement between 'dark'/'light'.

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: toggle the theme

fixtures._comment: Toggle Theme pill — 2nd leg: narration after the frontend tool runs. The probe passes on EITHER the html.dark class flip OR this 'Theme toggled' text landing (see _beautiful-chat-shared.assertToggleTheme two-track signal).

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: toggle the theme

fixtures._comment: Search Flights pill — 1st leg. langgraph's search_flights is a backend tool emitting an a2ui_operations container; hermes emits the middleware-injected render_a2ui directly with a flat FlightCard Row (identical shape to langgraph's _build_flight_components: a root Row whose children are per-flight FlightCard components with literal values). Two flights: United $349 / Delta $289 (the probe asserts United, Delta, $349, $289 are visible). airline is rendered verbatim by the FlightCard, so we use the short brand labels the probe expects. Catalog pinned by the route's defaultCatalogId (copilotkit://app-dashboard-catalog). Gated on hasToolResult:false; chunkSize:9999 emits the (large) args in one chunk so the A2UI binder sees the complete tree.

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: search flights from SFO to JFK

fixtures._comment: Search Flights pill — 2nd leg: one-sentence confirmation after the flight cards render. Gated on userMessage + hasToolResult:true rather than the render_a2ui toolCallId: the combined runtime's A2UI middleware feeds the render_a2ui result back to the model under a RUNTIME-generated tool_call_id (not the fixture's `call_d6_bc_search_flights_001`), so a toolCallId gate misses and the follow-up 404s (the demo then shows 'No fixture matched' and the surface never paints). hasToolResult:true is the stateless match for 'a tool result is now present', which flips this leg on for the follow-up while leg 1 (hasToolResult:false) only fires on the initial turn. Ordered ABOVE leg 1 so it wins once the render result is present.

match.userMessage: d5 beautiful-chat probe: search flights from SFO to JFK

response.content: Two flights shown above — United at $349 (08:00) and Delta at $289 (10:15), both on time.
